matches.match_rationale: Auditors currently capture findings in handwritten notes and unstructured PDFs — both explicitly named as pain sources. OCR and handwriting recognition can digitize these materials into machine-readable text, which is the essential first step toward a centralized repository. Without this digitization layer, paper-based audit evidence remains inaccessible to any downstream search or automation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A mobile-capture tool where auditors photograph handwritten notes or scan working papers on-campus; OCR/handwriting recognition converts them to structured text records stored centrally, with campus, date, and auditor metadata attached automatically.

matches.match_rationale: Email threads and OneNote exports contain audit findings but in inconsistent, semi-structured formats with no common schema. AI-assisted transformation maps these inputs to a standardized audit record schema (finding ID, campus, severity, owner, corrective action, status), enabling a unified repository. This directly addresses the 'scattered across email and OneNote' pain and the inability to demonstrate corrective action completion.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An ingestion pipeline that accepts email exports and OneNote HTML dumps, extracts audit finding records using AI schema mapping, deduplicates, and writes normalized records into a central compliance database — eliminating the manual re-documentation burden during external audits.

matches.match_rationale: When external auditors request re-documentation, staff spend significant hours reconstructing audit working papers from memory and scattered notes. Text generation can auto-draft standardized corrective action reports, audit finding summaries, and management response letters grounded in the structured records already captured. This converts re-documentation from a multi-hour task to a review-and-approve workflow.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Given a finding record (campus, finding type, severity, actions taken, dates), an LLM drafts a formatted audit response document meeting DepEd or accreditation body templates — staff review and sign rather than write from scratch.

matches.match_rationale: The core retrieval pain — 'audit evidence is hard to locate or verify' — is precisely what semantic search addresses. When a finding is disputed, staff need to surface the original working paper, follow-up notes, and corrective action evidence quickly. Keyword search fails because audit language is inconsistent across campuses; semantic search finds relevant records even when terminology differs.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance portal with a semantic search bar over the centralized audit repository: staff type 'safety drill non-compliance campus 3 2024' and retrieve all matching findings, evidence attachments, and corrective action records regardless of how they were originally worded.

matches.match_rationale: During DepEd inquiries or disciplinary proceedings, compliance officers need to quickly synthesize what actions were taken across multiple findings, campuses, and time periods — not just locate individual documents. RAG enables natural-language Q&A over the full audit corpus, generating cited, grounded responses like 'summarize all corrective actions taken for curriculum compliance findings at campus 2 in the past 18 months'.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A compliance Q&A assistant that answers auditor and legal team questions with citations to specific audit records, evidence attachments, and corrective action logs — enabling rapid, defensible responses to external inquiries without manual document assembly.

matches.match_rationale: The full audit lifecycle — capture finding, assign owner, track corrective action, collect evidence, close finding, archive — is a multi-step workflow currently executed manually across email chains. A task automation agent can orchestrate this entire process: parse incoming audit notes, create tracked findings, send assignment notifications, chase overdue corrective actions, and compile evidence packages. This directly eliminates the 'cannot demonstrate corrective actions were taken' risk.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An end-to-end audit management agent: when an auditor submits findings (photo, voice note, or form), the agent creates a tracked record, assigns a responsible campus manager, sends deadline reminders, collects uploaded evidence, marks findings closed, and assembles an audit trail package on demand — replacing the email-and-OneNote workflow entirely.
